response: The three main psychedelic compounds found in Psilocybin genus mushrooms that are converted to psilocin by the body are psilocybin, psilocin, and baeocystin. Psilocybin is converted to psilocin through metabolic processes in the body, while baeocystin is a phosphorylated derivative of 4-HO-NMT and is also converted to psilocin. Psilocin remains unchanged as it is already the active form.
